summary: Tested Meta (Facebook/Instagram) and Google Business Profile OAuth + publishing endpoints on backend, plus the SocialPublishPanel UI integration. All 15 backend pytest cases pass and the UI panel renders 3 'Connecter' buttons that correctly trigger /api/meta/login and /api/gbp/login. LinkedIn regression, drafts list, and draft editor are unaffected. OAuth end-to-end flow not testable because Meta/Google keys are dummy — expected per review request.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is >4500 lines — consider splitting Meta/GBP/LinkedIn OAuth into separate routers for maintainability

Meta redirect_uri.replace pattern (line 3028) is fragile — if the URI does not exactly contain '/api/meta/oauth/callback', the substitution silently fails; same issue on GBP (line 3186). Prefer computing frontend URL from a dedicated FRONTEND_URL env var
Callback endpoints raise HTTPException on invalid state; the returned JSON error is fine for tests but browsers see an ugly JSON page — consider RedirectResponse to /drafts?meta=error for better UX
gbp_status does not return refresh_token existence timestamp expiry — okay for MVP
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_social_publishing.py covers status, OAuth URL generation, invalid-state callbacks, publish error paths (401/400), disconnect idempotency, and LinkedIn/draft regression. Frontend Playwright verified panel + connect buttons trigger correct APIs. OAuth cannot go end-to-end because META_APP_ID/GBP_CLIENT_ID are dummies.
